# ID-32: OPS-T1 codemod implementation — wrap-define-route.ts ts-morph codemod

Implement `scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts`, a ts-morph utility that wraps the 137 mechanisable + 40 NEEDS-REVIEW routes identified in the route-shape inventory. The codemod applies dry-run + apply modes, infers ResponseSchema via type-drift baseline (Source A per TECH.md), and integrates with `lib/ast-dataflow/queries/type-drift-detect.ts` as a post-migration verifier. Implementation scope: (a) codemod authoring with dry-run + apply modes per PRODUCT.md; (b) ResponseSchema inference via type-drift baseline; (c) integration with type-drift-detect.ts as post-migration verifier; (d) test fixture set per Phase 1 shape variants.

Specs authored S11 R-WP-S11-A. Implementation aligned with main-track Phase 1 canonical-pipeline migration kickoff (ID-31).

### ID-32.6: Shape classifier

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** ID-32.5
- **Updated:** _unset_

Implement classifyRoute(sf: SourceFile): RouteShape function per TECH §2.3.

**Test strategy:** Vitest unit tests against synthetic ts-morph SourceFile objects for each of the 10 shape templates assert returned shape matches expected; titled 'classifies a single-method auth-wrapped handler with no params as AUTH_PLAIN', 'classifies a cron-secret route as CRON', etc.

**Details:**

Add classifyRoute() function and getExportedMethods() helper to scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts. Create scripts/codemods/types.ts with RouteShape discriminated union (AUTH_PLAIN | PARAM_BODY | BODY_VALIDATED | PARAM | MULTI_PARAM_BODY | MULTI_BODY | MULTI_PARAM | CRON | NAKED_NO_AUTH | MCP with +WRC sub-variants) and NeedsManualReason type (CRON_AUTH_MODEL | NAKED_NO_AUTH | MCP_TRANSPORT | MULTI_METHOD_SCHEMA | WRC_COMPOSITION | NEEDS_SCHEMA). Classifier inspects: imports (getImportDeclarations + getModuleSpecifierValue check for @/lib/auth + getAuthorisedClient/getAuthenticatedClient named imports); path-parameterisation ([ in path); body usage (request.json() or parseBody( in getFullText()); withRequestContext presence (getFullText().includes('withRequestContext')); exported methods (getExportedMethods returns names of FunctionDeclaration + VariableStatement-with-withRequestContext patterns). Per TECH §2.3 example code, classification priority: CRON > MCP > NAKED_NO_AUTH > multi-method > single-method variants.

### ID-32.8: ResponseSchema inference Source A (type-drift-baseline.json)

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** ID-32.7
- **Updated:** _unset_

Implement Source A inference per TECH §3.A — read docs/generated/type-drift-baseline.json, map interface name to route file via R-WP17 heuristic URL matcher, look up ${interfaceName}Schema or ${interfaceName}ZodSchema in lib/validation/schemas.ts. Also authors the 15th fixture (with-baseline-but-no-schema-constant.ts) exercising the fall-back path.

**Test strategy:** Vitest test against fixture with-schema-in-baseline.ts asserts inferSchema returns ReviewStatsResponseSchema (synthetic constant in mocked lib/validation/schemas.ts); negative test with-baseline-but-no-schema-constant.ts (authored by this Subtask) asserts returns z.unknown() + NEEDS_SCHEMA reason; titled 'inserts real schema when baseline interface has a co-located Schema constant', 'falls back to z.unknown() placeholder when the schema constant is missing'.

**Details:**

Add inferSchema(sf, method, project) function to scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts implementing Source A logic. Extract to scripts/codemods/inference-source-a.ts if baseline-loading + interface-to-route mapping exceeds 50 LOC. Read docs/generated/type-drift-baseline.json (existing 37-interface baseline shipped by R-WP17). For each baseline entry: (1) map interface name → route file via the heuristic URL matcher (re-use logic from lib/ast-dataflow/queries/type-drift-detect.ts — DO NOT modify that file); (2) look up ${interfaceName}Schema or ${interfaceName}ZodSchema in lib/validation/schemas.ts via name-convention; (3) if found, return schema identifier; (4) if not found, return z.unknown() + NEEDS_SCHEMA reason code for codemod-needs-manual.json. Per TECH §3.A trade-off, Zod schema naming conventions are not enforced — fall-back path is expected for some interfaces. AC-5 requires real schema for baseline routes WITH a discoverable Schema constant; placeholder path is acceptable per AC-6 (TODO comment). Authors 15th fixture __tests__/scripts/codemods/fixtures/wrap-define-route/with-baseline-but-no-schema-constant.ts (counterpart to 32.7's with-schema-in-baseline.ts; exercises the z.unknown() + NEEDS_SCHEMA fall-back).

<info added on 2026-05-22T13:43:43.000Z>
**Shipped:** Source A inference for `wrap-define-route` codemod. `inferSchema(sf, method, project, options?)` exported from `scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts`, delegating to `inferSchemaSourceA()` in the new `scripts/codemods/inference-source-a.ts` helper module. Algorithm reads `docs/generated/type-drift-baseline.json`, translates route file path → candidate URL, walks `lib/query/fetchers.ts` for `fetchJson<T>(url)` calls, filters by baseline-interface membership, then looks up `${T}Schema` or `${T}ZodSchema` in `lib/validation/schemas.ts`. Returns the schema identifier on hit; returns `z.unknown()` + `NEEDS_SCHEMA` reason on miss. Authors the 15th fixture (`with-baseline-but-no-schema-constant.ts`) exercising the fall-back path; bumps fixture-count guard 14 → 15 in the corpus harness.

**Architectural deviations (empirical findings per Q-EX2 discipline):**
- Extracted to `inference-source-b.ts` (sibling module) per the brief's 'extract to scripts/codemods/inference-source-b.ts if needed'. The brief's preferred default ('add function to wrap-define-route.ts') would have inflated the main module past the >800 LOC mark; honouring the sibling-extraction precedent set by 32.8 / 32.10 / 32.11 keeps the canonical `wrap-define-route.ts` import target intact via direct re-import — the chain composition still lives in `wrap-define-route.ts`'s `inferSchema()` per the brief.
- Chain order is **Source B → Source A** (Source B opportunistic, fall-through to A on null) rather than the brief's literal-reading 'chain Source A → Source B'. Rationale: the brief's testStrategy is explicit that 'Source B returns null when no annotation is present so Source A's result is used' — describing fall-through semantics. The semantic intuition is that an explicit developer annotation (Promise<NextResponse<X>>) beats Source A's heuristic URL matcher, because the annotation is unambiguous syntactic intent. TECH §3.B describes Source B as 'medium confidence' but the medium-vs-high comparison is for accuracy on the corpus level (Source A covers 37 routes vs Source B's ~2 routes per R-WP17) — when the annotation IS present, it is authoritative for that specific handler. Q-EX2 honour-the-spirit interpretation: the testStrategy describes B-first/A-fallback; the chain implements exactly that.
- `extractNextResponseTypeArg` accepts a wider set than the strict TECH §3.B sample: also recognises `NextResponse<X>` without the `Promise` wrapper (non-async edge case) and array-wrapped variants `Promise<NextResponse<X[]>>` and `Promise<NextResponse<ReadonlyArray<X>>>`. Mirrors Source A's fetcher walk where `fetchJson<X[]>` strips the array suffix before name-convention lookup — keeps the two sources' lookup targets symmetrical (always the bare interface name). The R-WP17 sample suggests only 2 corpus routes carry Source B annotations, but defensive coverage of the array forms is cheap and forward-compatible with any future annotated routes.
- The chain in `inferSchema()` forwards `options.schemasPath` to Source B but not `options.baseline` (Source B does not consume the baseline — it reads the annotation directly). The chain composition explicitly threads the `schemasPath` injection point so tests injecting an alternate schemas registry continue to work for both sources symmetrically.

**Follow-up flags for the Orchestrator:**
- Subtask 32.13 (idempotency check) is unaffected — `isAlreadyWrapped` is orthogonal to inference.
- Subtask 32.14 (apply mode) will dispatch the rewrite emitters (32.10 / 32.11) wired through `inferSchema()` — the chain now opportunistically picks up the 2 route-only annotated routes per R-WP17, plus any newly-annotated routes since the baseline was generated. The rewrite emitters' caller does not need to know which source produced the schema — both return the same `InferSchemaResult` shape.
- Source C remains explicitly out of scope per the brief / TECH §3.C / OQ-2. If post-32 backlog adds Source C, the chain extension lives in this same `inferSchema()` body — append a `if (sourceAResult.reason === 'NEEDS_SCHEMA') sourceCResult = ...` branch after the Source A call.

If the literal A → B order is preferred, the chain logic would invert (try A first, then fall through to B only when A returns NEEDS_SCHEMA); this would NOT change the test outcomes because the happy-path test deliberately empties Source A's input so only Source B can produce the result.

### ID-32.10: Handler rewrite — single-method shapes

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** ID-32.8
- **Updated:** _unset_

Implement rewrite logic per TECH §2.4 for AUTH_PLAIN, PARAM_BODY, BODY_VALIDATED, PARAM single-method shapes (137 MECHANISABLE routes). Includes withRequestContext sub-variant per TECH §8.1 / AC-7.

**Test strategy:** Vitest snapshot tests against each single-method fixture (auth-plain, auth-plain-with-wrc, param-body, body-validated, param-only) assert post-rewrite text matches inline snapshot; titles read as observable transformations: 'wraps an AUTH_PLAIN handler with defineRoute', 'preserves withRequestContext as the outer wrapper', 'preserves the Promise<params> second argument unchanged'.

**Details:**

Add rewriteSingleMethod(sf, method, schema) function to scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts; extract to scripts/codemods/rewrite-single-method.ts if exceeds 80 LOC. Targets FunctionDeclaration AST nodes with names in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS} ONLY — skips export const maxDuration / dynamic / runtime per TECH §8.3. Rewrites: 'export async function METHOD(req: NextRequest) { body }' → 'export const METHOD = defineRoute(Schema, async (req: NextRequest) => { body });'. Preserves Next.js 15 Promise<params> second argument per TECH §8.2 (78 of 92 parameterised routes use this shape). For withRequestContext sub-variant per TECH §8.1: outer wrapper order MUST be 'export const POST = withRequestContext(defineRoute(Schema, async (req) => { ... }))' — NEVER the reverse (defineRoute outside, withRequestContext inside) because request-context propagation depends on outer-wrap order. AC-7 explicitly asserts this. Also: add the 'import { defineRoute } from "@/lib/api/define-route"' import declaration if not present (use sf.getImportDeclaration check first per TECH §2.4 Step A). Place TODO comment '// TODO(OPS-T1): author ResponseSchema' on preceding line for routes inferring z.unknown() per AC-6.

### ID-32.13: Idempotency check

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** ID-32.10, ID-32.11
- **Updated:** _unset_

Implement already-wrapped detector per PRODUCT §4 — scan source file for top-level defineRoute(...) call inside export const METHOD = ... assignment; mark as SKIPPED, add to dry-run report, no entry in needs-manual.

**Test strategy:** Vitest test against fixture already-wrapped.ts asserts isAlreadyWrapped returns true and rewrite loop produces zero file modification (sf.print() === original text); second test runs apply mode twice consecutively against same fixture and asserts second run produces zero modifications; titled 'skips routes that already use defineRoute()' and 'second consecutive apply run produces no further modifications'.

**Details:**

Add isAlreadyWrapped(sf, method) function to scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts called at start of per-route rewrite loop. Implementation: inspect the VariableStatement / FunctionDeclaration matching the method name; check if its initialiser is a CallExpression with identifier 'defineRoute' at the top level (including the withRequestContext-wrapped form: withRequestContext(defineRoute(...))). If wrapped, mark route as SKIPPED, add 'api/items/[id]/route.ts — SKIPPED (already wrapped)' line to dry-run.md, do NOT add entry to codemod-needs-manual.json (skipped routes are not NEEDS-REVIEW or MANUAL). Idempotency guarantee per PRODUCT §4: re-running on already-wrapped route is no-op. Fixture: already-wrapped.ts from 32.7.

AC-7 composition); two new module-level constants (DEFINE_ROUTE_CALLEE_TEXT, WITH_REQUEST_CONTEXT_CALLEE_TEXT); buildRouteRecords() integration via `isIdempotentSkip` early branch (MANUAL bypass + all-methods predicate for partial-migration safety); file-level doc-string scope refresh through 32.13.

Total: 7 new tests (6 active, 1 skipped).

Test counts:
  - bun run test __tests__/scripts/codemods/ — 94 passed, 1 skipped (up from 88+1 at 32.9 close).
  - Targeted file: bun run test __tests__/scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.test.ts — 69 passed, 1 skipped (70 total).

Architectural decisions:
  - Brief's two options for the 'second consecutive apply run' assertion: (a) it.skip with TODO referencing 32.14, (b) implement minimal apply-loop helper. Chose (a) per brief recommendation — keeps 32.13 scope tight, defers apply-mode wiring to 32.14 where it belongs per dependency graph.
  - Brief's wording 'sf.print() === original text' for no-mutation assertion: dropped in favour of `sf.getFullText() === originalText`. ts-morph's SourceFile.print() re-emits through the TypeScript printer (canonicalises whitespace — multi-statement-per-line idioms split across lines, etc.) so it is NOT a faithful round-trip of the original source. The `already-wrapped.ts` fixture uses the production idiom `if (!cond) return ...` on a single line, which print() splits to two lines, so the literal print() assertion would falsely fail. The semantically correct invariant — 'no mutation occurred' — is `getFullText()` returning the verbatim original. Documented in the test's inline comment so the deviation from the brief's wording is visible to the Checker.
  - Partial-migration semantics: chose `methods.every(isAlreadyWrapped)` predicate (route is SKIPPED only when ALL exported methods are wrapped). A multi-method route where GET is wrapped but POST is not is NOT SKIPPED — the rewrite loop must still run on POST. Conservative — preserves AC-2 (apply-mode rewrites the un-wrapped methods).
  - MANUAL shapes (CRON / NAKED_NO_AUTH / MCP) bypass the idempotency check. The codemod never rewrites them, so they cannot be 'already wrapped' in the codemod's sense; the developer migrates them by hand under a different model. Running isAlreadyWrapped on MANUAL would be inert at best, misleading at worst.

Known out-of-scope test failures on production-readiness HEAD b4a04df6 (verified pre-existing via git-stash + run; NOT caused by 32.13):
  - __tests__/lib/ontology/markdown-parity.test.ts — ontology fixtures.

  - __tests__/validation/doc-freshness.test.ts — 'docs/operations/taxonomy-change-runbook.md is missing'.
  Recommend Orchestrator route these to the Curator/Checker per OOS-finding triage (4 distinct findings).

  - The existing CLI smoke test 'enumerates the route corpus and exits 0 in default dry-run mode' (193+ live routes) now silently exercises the idempotency gate against the production corpus. None of the 193 routes are currently wrapped (the codemod hasn't been run in apply mode yet) so the SKIPPED count is 0 in production. - **Status:** pending
- **Dependencies:** ID-32.13, ID-32.12
- **Updated:** _unset_

Wire sf.save() into rewrite loop behind --apply flag. After all routes saved, run bun run format against modified file set per TECH §8.5.

**Test strategy:** Vitest test runs wrap-define-route.ts --apply against temp copy of fixture corpus and asserts: each MECHANISABLE fixture file has changed on disk; each NEEDS-REVIEW fixture has had each exported method rewritten; each MANUAL fixture is bit-identical to its original; titled 'writes 137 MECHANISABLE routes to disk under --apply' and 'leaves MANUAL routes bit-identical under --apply'.

**Details:**

Add applyAll(routes) function and runFormatPass(modifiedFilePaths) helper to scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts. In dry-run mode, sf.save() skipped — only sf.print() vs original text computed for diff preview. In apply mode (--apply flag), iterate over MECHANISABLE + NEEDS-REVIEW routes, invoke sf.save() per file. After save loop completes, run 'bun run format' against the modified file paths (use child_process.spawnSync with the path list as args) OR invoke ts-morph's sf.organizeImports() before save. Per TECH §8.5, the import-ordering pass is needed because ts-morph's addImportDeclaration may insert at a non-Prettier-compatible position. Per AC-8 ('bun run test' passes after apply) and AC-9 ('bun lint' reports 0 errors), the format pass also reduces lint surface.

### ID-32.17: Classifier `getFullText().includes()` → AST refactor (pre-empt 32.16 acceptance gate)

- **Status:** done
- **Dependencies:** ID-32.7
- **Updated:** _unset_

Replace classifyRoute() body-detection getFullText().includes() substring scan in scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts with ts-morph CallExpression walk filtered by getExpression().getText() match. Restricts detection to executable code only, eliminating the latent JSDoc/comment false-positive risk surfaced by S64 W1 Checker on Subtask 32.7. Extends fixture corpus with JSDoc-poisoned PARAM/MULTI_PARAM regression case. Pre-empts 32.16 acceptance gate per S62E sub-o 1 brief.

**Test strategy:** Extend __tests__/scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.classifier.test.ts with JSDoc-poisoned PARAM and MULTI_PARAM fixtures asserting classifyRoute correctly returns PARAM/MULTI_PARAM (not BODY-tainted variants) when discriminator substrings appear only in JSDoc/comments. Maintains 32.6 PASS 24/24 + 32.7 PASS 39/39 baselines under refactor.

**Details:**

classifyRoute() currently uses getFullText().includes('request.json()') and getFullText().includes('parseBody(') to classify route body shape. This scans comments and JSDoc as well as executable code, creating a latent false-positive: any route file (fixture or production) that mentions discriminator substrings in JSDoc/inline comments will be misclassified. The 32.7 fixture corpus worked around this by deliberately omitting those substrings from JSDoc bodies on PARAM/MULTI_PARAM fixtures. Fix: replace the substring scan with sf.getDescendantsOfKind(SyntaxKind.CallExpression) filtered by getExpression().getText() match — restricts detection to executable code only, per ts-morph conventions already used in the codemod surface. Extend unit test fixtures with at least one JSDoc-poisoned PARAM/MULTI_PARAM fixture to prevent regression. Detection cost is high if deferred to the 32.16 production-route acceptance gate (137 mechanisable + 40 needs-review routes may carry JSDoc mentioning these substrings). Surfaced: S64 W1 sub-o Checker on Subtask 32.7 (commit b039e1b1). Impl ref commit: 7c6374ed (Subtask 32.6). File: scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts — classifyRoute() body-detection branch.
